m_Item1.QuestionText: A mathematical-model with a collection of operations defined on that model is called
m_Item1.Explanation: In computer science, an abstract data type (ADT) is a mathematical model for data types, where a data type is defined by its behavior (semantics) from the point of view of a user of the data, specifically in terms of possible values, possible operations on data of this type, and the behavior of these operations.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If the address of A[1][1] and A[2][1] are 1000 and 1010 respectively and each element occupies 2 bytes then the array has been stored in _________ order.
m_Item1.Explanation:
In a row-major order, elements which are stored in adjacent locations constitute a row, whereas in a column-major order the elements which are stored in adjacent locations constitute a column.

Coming to the above example, if the order was column-major order, then a[1][1] and a[2][1] would be adjacent memory locations. However since &a[1][1] is 1000 and &a[2][1] is 1010, clearly this is not column-major order.
Therefore the answer should be row-major, with number of columns equaling 4, ie, array[] of array[4] of int.

m_Item1.QuestionText: O<sub>(N)</sub> (linear time) is better than O<sub>(1)</sub> constant time.
m_Item1.Explanation: O(1) is constant-time, which means that the operation does not depend up on the input size, and O(n) is linear time, which means that the operation changes linearly with input size.

m_Item1.QuestionText: An ADT is defined to be a mathematical model of a user-defined type along with the collection of all ____________ operations on that model.
m_Item1.Explanation: In computer science, an abstract data type (ADT) is a mathematical model for data types where a data type is defined by its behavior (semantics) from the point of view of a user of the data, specifically in terms of possible values, possible operations on data of this type, and the behavior of these operations.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The extra key inserted at the end of the array is called a,
m_Item1.Explanation:
The sentinel value typically is chosen so as to not be a similar data value that the loop will encounter and attempt to perform with. For example, in a loop algorithm that computes non-negative integers, the value "-1" can be set as the sentinel value as the computation will never encounter that value as a similar processing output.
Also referred to as a flag value or a signal value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following data structure is not linear data structure?
m_Item1.Explanation: Array,Stack & Linked list all  are linear data structures.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Term Data Structure refers to _________ and interrelationship between them
m_Item1.Explanation: Datastructure is way to organization & retrival of data in a efficient way.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following Data Structure is/are linear Data Structures?
m_Item1.Explanation: String is also array of char, list & queue are also list/array, so all are linear.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The organization and management of data structures take place in:
m_Item1.Explanation: Current program data members will be organised or created on primary/main memory which is used by current program.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
#include<iostream>
using namespace std;
void main() {
char p[]="This is a test";
cout<<sizeof(p)<<","<<strlen(p);
}
m_Item1.Explanation: Sizeof array is actual number of charecters plus one that is for  '' so sizeof(p) is 15.  And string length is 14 which is actual number of character .

m_Item1.QuestionText: When new data are to be inserted into a data structure, but there is no available space; this situation is usually called
m_Item1.Explanation: When any data structure exceed the size limit,. This situaltion is Overflow.

m_Item1.QuestionText: An algorithm that calls itself directly or indirectly is known as
m_Item1.Explanation: The process in which a function calls itself directly or indirectly is called recursion and the corresponding function is called as recursive function.

m_Item1.QuestionText: ADT is called as Abstract because

Options.OptionText: It is completely independent data type
It is collection of different data types

m_Item1.Explanation: Abstract data type are those whose complex(implementation) part is hidden & only data & operation on that data is visible.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In linear data structure each and every element can/may have ____ predecessor and _____ successor.
m_Item1.Explanation: In linear data structure every element is  stored on next successive address, 1 element after every element & it will be unique.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Mathematical Model that can have set of operations that can be performed on that model is called as _________.
m_Item1.Explanation: Abstract data types will be those that are actual data & some operations that can be performed on the data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In _________ data structure , data contain hierarchical and network relationship between elements.
m_Item1.Explanation: Non linear data structures contain data in hierarchical/network type.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is non-linear data structure?
m_Item1.Explanation: Stack ,Queue and linked list these are linear data structures , and trees and graph non linear data structures

m_Item1.QuestionText: The operation of processing each element in the list is known as
m_Item1.Explanation: Processing each element is nothing but visiting or traversing the array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Recursion uses more memory space than iteration because

Options.OptionText: it uses Queue instead of Stack.
every recursive call has to be stored.
None of the above are true.

m_Item1.Explanation: Recursion uses stack but the main reason is, every recursive call needs to be stored separately in the memory.

m_Item1.QuestionText: O(N2)  is better than O(log n)
m_Item1.Explanation: False because O(log n ) is faster than O(n^2). The O(log n) notationn represents logarithmic time complexity, which grows at a much slower rate thean O(n^2),  which represents quadratic time complexity. Therefore, algorithms with O(log n) complexity are generally more efficient than those with O(n^2) complexity for large input sizes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When new data are to be inserted into a data structure, but there is no available space; this situation is usually called
m_Item1.Explanation: When any data structure exceed the size limit,. This situaltion is Overflow.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In _________ data structure , data contain hierarchical and network relationship between elements.
m_Item1.Explanation: Non linear data structures contain data in either hierarchical/network type.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following  is true about characteristics of abstract data type
    I. It exports a Type
    II. It exports a set of operations.
m_Item1.Explanation: ADT is having it's own data members and it's own fucntion, set of rules . So both statements are true.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Representation of data structure in memory is known as:
m_Item1.Explanation: Abstract Data type (ADT) is a type (or class) for objects whose behavior is defined by a set of values and a set of operations. The definition of ADT only mentions what operations are to be performed but not how these operations will be implemented. It does not specify how data will be organized in memory and what algorithms will be used for implementing the operations. It is called “abstract” because it gives an implementation-independent view.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The largest element of an array index is called its
Which data structure is used in breadth first search of a graph to hold nodes?
m_Item1.Explanation: BFS or breadth first search is levelwise traversal, so it required Queue Data Sturcture.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The smallest element of an array’s index is called its
m_Item1.Explanation: Lower bound is something where array index is 0.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is true about an abstract data type?

Options.OptionText: It is a data structure that contains only abstract methods
It is a logical description of how we view the data and its operations
It refers to any data type defined by the user
It is a primitive data type provided by the programming language

m_Item1.Explanation: An abstract data type (ADT) describes a model of data that specifies what operations are possible on that data, but not how these operations are implemented. This abstraction helps in focusing on 'what' operations are done rather than 'how' they are done.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary benefit of using abstract data types?

Options.OptionText: They provide faster execution of code
They make programs easier to understand by hiding implementation details

m_Item1.Explanation: ADTs help in encapsulating the details about how the data is stored and operated upon, leading to cleaner and more easily understandable code by allowing programmers to focus on high-level structure.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In Java, which of these can be considered an example of an abstract data type?
m_Item1.Explanation: LinkedList in Java is an example of an abstract data type. It provides a concept of a list with operations such as insertion, deletion, and traversal without the need for understanding the inner implementations.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following operations typically cannot be performed on an abstract data type directly?
m_Item1.Explanation: An abstract data type hides the details of its implementation and storage mechanism. The primary operations defined do not include understanding or directly interacting with the underlying mechanism.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In Java, which method can be used to convert a byte array into a String?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'new String(byte[] data)' constructor creates a new String object by decoding the specified array of bytes using the platform's default charset.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following correctly retrieves the length of an array 'arr' declared in Java?
m_Item1.Explanation: In Java, arrays have a 'length' property that stores the number of elements in the array. It is accessed using 'arr.length'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will happen if you try to access an index that is out of the bounds for a declared array in Java?

Options.OptionText: The program will compile but throw an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException at runtime.
The program will compile and automatically resize the array.
The program will replace the out of bounds index with the last valid index.

m_Item1.Explanation: In Java, accessing an index outside the array bounds results in an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException at runtime.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If 'int[] numbers = new int[5];' is declared in Java, what are the initial values in this array?
m_Item1.Explanation: In Java, when an array is instantiated with 'new', its elements are automatically initialized to the default value of the data type, which is 0 for integers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method can be used to fill an array with a static value in Java?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'Arrays.fill()' method is used to assign the specified value to each element of the specified range of the array.
The element at index 2 is 3 and the element at index 4 is 5. Their sum is 3 + 5 = 8.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Given the array: int[] numbers = {2, 3, 8, 4, 1}; What will numbers.length return?
m_Item1.Explanation: The length of the array 'numbers' is 5 because it contains 5 elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Consider the code: int[] nums = new int[5]; nums[5] = 10; What will happen when this code runs?

Options.OptionText: It will compile and run without error
It will assign 10 to the last index of the array
It will cause a syntax error

m_Item1.Explanation: The array 'nums' has indexes 0 to 4. Accessing index 5 is out of bounds, leading to an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you correctly initialize a multidimensional array in Java?
m_Item1.Explanation: To initialize a 2D array in Java, you can use the syntax 'int[][] matrix = {{1, 2}, {3, 4}};'.
The sum of the array elements 2, 4, 6, and 8 is 2 + 4 + 6 + 8 = 20.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a characteristic of a linear data structure?

Options.OptionText: Data elements are arranged in hierarchical manner.
There are multiple levels of storage.
It requires multiple pointers for data access.

m_Item1.Explanation: In linear data structures, data elements are arranged in a sequential order, such as in arrays or linked lists.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following operations is used to insert an element at the end of a singly linked list?
m_Item1.Explanation: The AddLast() operation inserts an element at the end of a singly linked list.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the time complexity of accessing an element in an array by index?
m_Item1.Explanation: Accessing an element by index in an array has a time complexity of O(1) because it directly accesses memory location using index.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is used to add an element at a specific position in a Java ArrayList?
m_Item1.Explanation: The add(index, element) method is used to insert an element at a specific position in a Java ArrayList.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a stack data structure, which principle is followed to access elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: A stack follows the Last In, First Out (LIFO) principle, meaning the last element added is the first one to be removed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a characteristic of a non-linear data structure as opposed to a linear data structure?

Options.OptionText: Elements are stored in a sequential manner.
Data elements can be organized in hierarchical relationships.
Each element is connected to only two other elements.
The data structure has a fixed size.

m_Item1.Explanation: Non-linear data structures like trees and graphs allow elements to be connected in complex hierarchical ways, unlike linear data structures such as arrays and linked lists which store elements sequentially.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a binary tree, which Java class can be used to represent each node effectively?
m_Item1.Explanation: A TreeNode class in Java typically represents a node in a binary tree, containing data and references to left and right child nodes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a key advantage of using a graph data structure in Java?

Options.OptionText: They allow representation of complex relationships between data elements.
Graphs use less memory than arrays.

m_Item1.Explanation: Graphs are versatile and can model complex relationships between entities, which is useful in scenarios such as social networks, transportation networks, etc.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which Java interface would you implement for a class designed to create a priority queue?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Queue interface in Java is designed for handling collection of elements in a specific order (such as a priority order), which is implemented by priority queues.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which data structure is most suitable for implementing a LRU (Least Recently Used) cache in Java?

Options.OptionText: HashMap with a doubly linked list

m_Item1.Explanation: A combination of a HashMap and a doubly linked list allows for efficient O(1) time complexity for both data insertion and retrieval, which is crucial for implementing an LRU cache.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which data structure would you prefer to implement a call-center routing system where you need to handle high-priority calls efficiently?
m_Item1.Explanation: A priority queue allows for calls to be handled based on their priority levels, making it ideal for routing high-priority calls promptly.

m_Item1.QuestionText: For implementing undo functionality in an application, which data structure should be considered?
m_Item1.Explanation: A stack is the most suitable data structure for undo functionality as it follows the LIFO (Last In, First Out) principle, which naturally supports reversing actions in order.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When dealing with a large dataset where frequent searching is required, which data structure should be used to achieve relatively fast search times?
m_Item1.Explanation: A HashMap provides average-time complexity of O(1) for search operations using hash functions, making it the preferable data structure for frequent and fast searches in large datasets.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which data structure is recommended for an application with a requirement of maintaining a constantly sorted list of elements for instant retrieval?
m_Item1.Explanation: A TreeSet in Java maintains a sorted set of elements via a Red-Black tree, which enables instant sorting and efficient retrieval, satisfying the need to maintain a sorted collection.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Why are data structures needed in programming?

Options.OptionText: To organize and store data efficiently for easy access and modification

m_Item1.Explanation: Data structures help in organizing, storing, and managing data efficiently, which allows faster access and modification. They form the foundation for building efficient algorithms and solving complex problems effectively.
